assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 21-29 detail all 5 criteria: single license filter, comma-separated multi-license filter, invalid license returns 400, pagination integration, and response shape preserved. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' for the corresponding criterion.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies `modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs` and `modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs`, and creates `tests/api/package.rs` -- exactly matching the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create lists'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). The report and criterion files contain only analysis and verification findings, with no indication of code modifications. All findings reference reading and analyzing code ('The diff shows...', 'The PR implements...') without any edits.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a detailed summary of findings. The footer reads 'This report was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr'. There is no mention of merging, approving, or any automated action beyond reporting. The report presents findings for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` adds 4 test functions covering single filter, multi-filter, invalid input, and pagination integration'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains all required verdicts: Intent Alignment domain — Scope Containment (line 7: PASS), Diff Size (line 8: PASS), Commit Traceability (line 9: PASS); Security domain — Sensitive Patterns (line 10: PASS); Correctness domain — CI Status (line 11: PASS), Acceptance Criteria (line 12: PASS), Verification Commands (line 15: N/A); Style/Conventions domain — Test Quality (line 13: PASS, combining 'no repetitive tests detected', 'All 4 test functions have doc comments', and 'Eval Quality: N/A'), Test Change Classification (line 14: ADDITIVE).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: report.md line 7 lists specific files ('modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs', 'tests/api/package.rs') and compares against task's Files to Modify/Create lists. Security: line 10 states 'No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Correctness: Lines 21-29 provide per-criterion code-level evidence (function names like 'validate_license_param', type names like 'AppError::BadRequest', test function names, specific assertions). criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive code-level verification per criterion with file paths, function names, and logic flow. Style/Conventions: line 13 details 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (each test covers a distinct scenario with different setup and assertions)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (each test covers a distinct scenario with different setup and assertions); Eval Quality: N/A'. Eval Quality is explicitly marked as N/A within the Test Quality row.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. Root-Cause Investigation is N/A with 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate' (report.md line 6), confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear at the top of the table (lines 5-6), before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), consistent with being determined independently by the orchestrator.
